mandatory_concerns_addressed.active_particle_correction: Addressed in H1 (p_c shift to 0.21-0.24), H6 (self-trapping), H7 (MSD exponent at p_c). Pe ~ 3 acknowledged throughout. 2D-only data flagged.
mandatory_concerns_addressed.ashworth_2015_scoping: Addressed in H1 novelty section: four specific differences (tumor immunology, T cells as active particles, full critical phenomena, LOX as molecular actuator).
mandatory_concerns_addressed.kegg_gap: Acknowledged: LOX-immune link relies on 2024-2025 literature (PMID 38267662, 38305736, 39101793) not yet in KEGG.

hypotheses.title: LOX Crosslink Density as Bond Occupation Probability Predicts a Universal Immune Exclusion Threshold at p ~ 0.21-0.25
hypotheses.connection: 3D bond percolation threshold → LOX-mediated collagen crosslink density as bond occupation probability → T cell immune exclusion phase transition
hypotheses.why_wrong: ECM not random lattice; chemotaxis may override topology; LOX-to-p mapping nonlinear; 2D-to-3D active correction unvalidated
hypotheses.literature_gap: No paper applies percolation critical phenomena to T cell infiltration in tumor ECM. No threshold identified for LOX-mediated immune exclusion.

hypotheses.title: Correlation Length Exponent nu = 0.88 Predicts T Cell Cluster Size Distribution Near Hot-Cold Boundary
hypotheses.connection: Percolation correlation length scaling → Spatial correlation of T cell exclusion zones → Parameter-free prediction (tau ~ 2.19) for spatial transcriptomics
hypotheses.why_wrong: Most tumors far from critical point; chemokines/antigen/vasculature drive clustering too; lattice constant ambiguous; power laws non-unique
hypotheses.literature_gap: No analysis of T cell spatial distributions through percolation theory. Hot/cold classification lacks quantitative physical model.

hypotheses.title: Finite-Size Scaling Explains Discordant Infiltration Scores Between Biopsies and Resections
hypotheses.connection: Finite-size scaling in percolation → Biopsy sampling artifacts → Immunoscore calibration
hypotheses.why_wrong: Discordance driven by antigen/signaling heterogeneity not ECM; correlated ECM density violates random assumption; lattice constant ill-defined
hypotheses.literature_gap: Biopsy discordance attributed to generic heterogeneity. No quantitative framework predicting when discordance peaks.

hypotheses.title: Collagen I/III Ratio Acts as Lattice Topology Switch Shifting p_c, Explaining Macrophage-Mediated Cold-to-Hot Conversion
hypotheses.connection: Percolation threshold dependence on lattice topology → Collagen I (aligned) vs III (intermingled) as different lattices → Macrophage-Tcf4 axis as p_c modulator
hypotheses.why_wrong: Collagen types differ in stiffness/integrin binding not just topology; macrophage depletion has many non-ECM effects; mixed networks not cleanly separable
hypotheses.literature_gap: Fusilier 2025 showed topology controls infiltration but no quantitative framework. No percolation analysis of Collagen I vs III networks.

hypotheses.title: LOX Inhibitor Dose-Response Follows Order Parameter Scaling P_inf ~ (p-p_c)^0.417, Predicting Therapeutic Window
hypotheses.connection: Percolation order parameter scaling → LOX inhibitor pharmacology → Therapeutic window below maximum tolerated dose
hypotheses.why_wrong: LOX inhibition affects stiffness/mechanotransduction beyond topology; dose-to-p mapping nonlinear; BAPN not LOX-specific
hypotheses.literature_gap: LOX inhibitor trials assume monotonic dose-response. No percolation-based dose-response model exists for immunotherapy combinations.

hypotheses.title: Active T Cell Self-Trapping Near Percolation Threshold Explains Paradoxical Margin Accumulation
hypotheses.connection: Active Brownian particle self-trapping → Pe-dependent T cell motility inversion → Activated T cells MORE excluded than exhausted T cells near p_c
hypotheses.why_wrong: Self-trapping requires Pe >> 10 (T cells Pe ~ 3); T cells deformable unlike rigid ABPs; margin accumulation has immunological explanations; amoeboid not persistent motion
hypotheses.literature_gap: No application of active particle percolation to T cell migration. Self-trapping prediction for immune exclusion is entirely novel.

hypotheses.title: Universality Class Exponents Are Tumor-Type-Invariant, Enabling Pan-Cancer Percolation Model
hypotheses.connection: Percolation universality → Identical critical exponents across PDAC, breast, lung → Single (p, p_c) model for immunotherapy response prediction
hypotheses.why_wrong: Correlated disorder (Harris criterion) may shift universality class; fiber alignment → directed percolation class; chemotaxis creates biased walk; finite tumor size limits
hypotheses.literature_gap: No pan-cancer framework predicts T cell infiltration from ECM physics. Hot/cold classification is empirical with no parameter-free predictions.

hypotheses.title: TGF-beta-LOX Feedback as Bond-Correlated Percolation Predicts Super-Additive LOX + Anti-TGF-beta Synergy
hypotheses.connection: Correlated percolation theory → TGF-beta-collagen positive feedback as spatial bond correlation → p_c de-correlation predicts drug synergy
hypotheses.why_wrong: TGF-beta signaling range may be too short for lattice-scale correlation; TGF-beta blockade has many non-ECM immune effects; Harris criterion may make correlation irrelevant; feedback may push system far from p_c
hypotheses.literature_gap: LOX + anti-TGF-beta synergy analyzed by Bliss/Loewe models. No percolation-based synergy mechanism via threshold de-correlation proposed.
